folio.label: 72v
folio.l: A likerous moutú , must han a likerous tail
In womman vynolent , is no diffence
This knowen lecchours , by ex¹ience
But lord crist , whan that it remembretú me
Vp on my ©outhe , and on my Iolyte
It tikeletú me , a boute myn hert rote
Vn to this day , it dootú myn herte bote
That I haue had my world , as in my tyme
But age allas , that al wyl envenyme
Hatú me byraft , my beaute and my pitú
Late go fare wel , the deuele goo there witú
The flour is goon , there is nomore to telle
The bren as I beest can , now mot I selle
But ©et to be rigút merye , wol I fonde
¢ Now fortú to telle , of my secund husbonde
I sey I had , in hert greet despit´
That he of any other , had delit´
But he was quyt , be god and be Seynt Ioce
I mad hi~ of the same wode , a Croce
Nat of my body , in no foul manere
But certeynly , I made folk swicú chere
That in his owen grees , I mad hi~ frie
For anger , and for verrey Ielusie

note.notetxt: 488 Two crosses in the left margin were rubbed off. The parchement looks comsumed in that point.ODR

folio.l: By god , in erthe , I was his purgatorie
For whiche I hope , his soule be in glorie
For god it woot , he sat ful ofte and songe
Whan that his shoo , ful bitterly hi~ wronge
For there was no wygút , saue god and he æ wyst´
In manye wyses , how sore I hi~ twist´
He deyed , whan I come , fro Ierusalem
And litú in graue , vnder the roode Bemö
Al is his toumbe , nougút so curious
As was the sepulcre , of hi~ Darius
Whiche that Appollus , wrougút´ so sotelly
It nys but waast , b b to bery hi~ preciously
Lat hi~ fare wel , god ©eue his soule good reest
He is now in his gue , and ded in his chest
¢ Now of my thridde husbonde , wol I telle
God lat his soule , neuäe come in helle
And ©et was he to me , the moost shrewe
That fele I now on my ribbes , al by rewe
And euäe shal , vn to myn endyng day
But in oure bed , he was ful fressú and gay
¢ And there witú also , wel coude he me gloose
Whan that he wolde , han my beal chose
